Question: What are the primary business challenges driving the AI recommendation initiative?
Guidance: Describe the top pain points — e.g., poor content discovery, high churn, low engagement with new uploads
Notes: Understanding root-cause drivers ensures the technical solution targets real business outcomes

Question: How are users currently discovering new songs or podcasts within the ANP Streaming app?
Guidance: Manual search, editor-curated playlists, word-of-mouth, social sharing, etc.
Notes: Establishes the baseline experience the AI solution must improve upon

Question: What measurable outcomes define success for this project?
Guidance: Examples: 20% increase in playlist engagement, 50% reduction in manual tagging time, improved user retention rate
Notes: Critical for scoping the solution and evaluating post-launch performance

Question: How will you measure the quality of mood classification applied to new uploads?
Guidance: Accuracy threshold, manual spot-check cadence, user feedback loop, A/B testing
Notes: Drives model evaluation strategy and any human-review workflow requirements

Question: Is the 4–6 week engagement timeline fixed or flexible?
Guidance: Identify any hard deadlines — e.g., product launch date, marketing campaign, investor demo
Notes: Tight timelines may require scope reduction or additional resources

Question: What are the three review milestones and expected feedback turnaround?
Guidance: Confirm the three-phase approach: discovery/architecture, build, integration/handoff
Notes: Validates phased delivery plan and aligns expectations on review cycles

Question: Is the ~$15,000 professional-services budget fixed or is there flexibility for scope changes?
Guidance: Clarify approval process for change orders and budget authority
Notes: Scope management and change-order planning

Question: Are there any active AWS funding programs (MAP credits, APN credits, Activate credits) that should be applied to this engagement?
Guidance: Check AWS Migration Acceleration Program or Startup credits available to ANP
Notes: Could offset AWS build and ongoing infrastructure costs significantly

Question: Who are the key stakeholders who must approve architecture decisions and deliverables?
Guidance: Confirm Lilly Goyah as executive sponsor; identify any technical approvers or board members
Notes: Prevents last-minute escalations and approval delays

Question: What is the involvement level of ANP's technical point of contact during the engagement?
Guidance: Hours per week available, responsibilities (data access, API testing, catalog export)
Notes: Determines how much ANP-side effort is needed and where blockers may arise

Question: Are there faith-based content guidelines or editorial policies that the AI must respect when classifying or recommending content?
Guidance: E.g., mood labels must align with Christian values; certain content categories must never be recommended together
Notes: May require custom label taxonomy or post-processing filters on AI outputs

Question: What is the current structure and size of the Firebase catalog?
Guidance: Approximate number of songs and podcasts; fields available: titles, artists, tags, lyric/transcript text, upload date
Notes: Primary input data for both the classifier and recommendation engine

Question: In what format is lyric and transcript text stored in Firebase?
Guidance: Plain text, HTML, structured JSON, third-party captions (e.g., Rev.ai), character encoding
Notes: Impacts text pre-processing pipeline design and NLP service selection

Question: What is the average and maximum length of lyric or transcript text per item?
Guidance: Character count or word count ranges; are podcasts full transcripts or summaries?
Notes: Drives token-limit considerations for LLM-based classification and API payload sizing

Question: How does the FlutterFlow app currently authenticate with Firebase — and what auth mechanism should be used for the new AWS REST API?
Guidance: Firebase Auth (JWT), API key, OAuth 2.0, AWS Cognito — identify what the app can pass today
Notes: Authentication design determines API Gateway authorizer type and security posture

Question: What HTTP method and payload structure does the mood-classification endpoint need to accept?
Guidance: POST with JSON body containing lyric/transcript text; confirm field names expected by the app team
Notes: API contract definition required before build phase begins

Question: What HTTP method and payload structure does the personalized playlist endpoint need to accept?
Guidance: POST or GET with mood label and recent listening history (track IDs, timestamps); confirm array size limits
Notes: Drives request validation logic and Lambda payload design

Question: What response format and fields does the FlutterFlow app expect from each endpoint?
Guidance: JSON structure: mood label + confidence score for classifier; ordered track IDs + metadata for playlist
Notes: API contract must be finalised before FlutterFlow integration to avoid rework

Question: Are there any AWS region preferences or restrictions for deploying the solution?
Guidance: Primary region (e.g., us-east-1, us-west-2); any data-residency requirements
Notes: Region selection impacts latency to Firebase and AWS service availability

Question: Does ANP already have an AWS account, or will a new account be created for this engagement?
Guidance: Confirm account ID and that nClouds has admin IAM access; single account or multi-account (dev/prod)
Notes: Account setup is a Day-1 prerequisite; multi-account adds complexity

Question: Which mood or emotion taxonomy should the classifier use?
Guidance: Predefined label set (e.g., Joyful, Peaceful, Reflective, Uplifting, Lament) vs. open-ended generation
Notes: Custom label set may be required to align with ANP's faith-based content philosophy

Question: What AWS AI/ML services are preferred or approved for this solution?
Guidance: Amazon Bedrock (foundation models), Amazon Comprehend, custom SageMaker model, or combination
Notes: Service selection drives cost, latency, accuracy, and compliance posture

Question: Is there an existing labeled dataset of songs/podcasts with mood tags that can be used to validate or fine-tune the classifier?
Guidance: Number of labeled examples, label quality, who created the labels
Notes: Determines whether zero-shot, few-shot, or fine-tuned approach is most appropriate

Question: What is the expected volume of new uploads per day/week that require mood classification?
Guidance: Current upload rate and projected growth rate over 12 months
Notes: Drives throughput sizing for the classification pipeline and cost projection

Question: What listening-history signals are available per user for generating recommendations?
Guidance: Track plays, skips, likes, playlist saves, session duration — and how far back history is retained
Notes: Determines recommendation algorithm complexity and cold-start handling strategy

Question: What is the maximum acceptable latency for the playlist recommendation endpoint?
Guidance: Target p95 response time — e.g., under 500 ms, under 2 seconds
Notes: Drives architecture choice: synchronous Lambda vs. pre-computed recommendations cached in DynamoDB or ElastiCache

Question: What is the maximum acceptable latency for the mood-classification endpoint?
Guidance: Acceptable if synchronous (user waiting) or can it be asynchronous (background job)?
Notes: Async classification allows cheaper batch processing; sync requires low-latency LLM or cached results

Question: What is the expected number of concurrent API users at launch and within 12 months?
Guidance: Concurrent sessions peak estimate — used for Lambda concurrency and API Gateway throttling settings
Notes: Right-sizes auto-scaling configuration and reserved concurrency allocation

Question: How will Firebase listening-history data be made available to the AWS recommendation endpoint?
Guidance: Direct Firebase REST query at call time, periodic sync to DynamoDB, or webhook-driven replication
Notes: Integration pattern choice affects latency, cost, and data freshness

Question: Does the solution need to write mood labels back to Firebase after classification?
Guidance: Confirm whether AWS will push labels to Firebase via REST or whether the app team pulls from AWS
Notes: Bidirectional integration increases scope; clarify data-ownership boundaries

Question: Are there any third-party services (CDN, analytics, CMS) that must be considered in the API design?
Guidance: e.g., Cloudflare, Segment, Contentful — any that sit between FlutterFlow and the new API
Notes: Identifies potential routing, CORS, or token-passthrough issues

Question: Are there any compliance frameworks that must be satisfied for handling user data?
Guidance: CCPA, COPPA (if users under 13), GDPR (if serving EU users), SOC 2 plans — specify applicability
Notes: Faith-based app may have minor-aged users; COPPA has strict data-handling rules

Question: What is the sensitivity level of the data the API will process?
Guidance: Lyric/transcript text (content), user listening history (behavioral PII), account identifiers
Notes: Determines encryption, logging, and retention requirements

Question: What authentication mechanism will secure the new AWS REST API from the FlutterFlow app?
Guidance: API key, AWS Cognito JWT, Firebase Auth token verified by Lambda authorizer, IAM SigV4
Notes: Unauthorized access to the recommendation API could expose user behavioral data

Question: Will the API be accessible only from the mobile app, or also from a web dashboard or admin tooling?
Guidance: Identify all consumers of the API and their auth capabilities
Notes: Multiple consumers may require different authorizer strategies per endpoint

Question: Are there requirements for encrypting data at rest and in transit?
Guidance: TLS 1.2+ in transit (API Gateway default); at-rest encryption for DynamoDB, S3, CloudWatch Logs
Notes: Baseline AWS encryption should be confirmed and documented for any compliance audit

Question: Are there restrictions on where user behavioral data (listening history) may be stored or processed?
Guidance: US-only, specific AWS regions, or no restriction
Notes: Data residency requirements constrain region selection and cross-region replication

Question: What audit logging is required for API calls and AI model invocations?
Guidance: CloudTrail for AWS API actions; custom access logs in API Gateway; Bedrock/model invocation logs
Notes: Logging scope affects storage costs and compliance evidence availability

Question: Who within ANP and nClouds needs access to the AWS account, and at what permission level?
Guidance: Admin, developer, read-only — separate roles for build phase vs. steady-state operations
Notes: Least-privilege IAM design; important for handoff and ongoing operations

Question: Are there requirements for code scanning, dependency audits, or penetration testing before go-live?
Guidance: SAST tools, OWASP checks, third-party pen test, AWS Inspector
Notes: Determines additional pre-launch security activities and timeline impact

Question: What is the maximum acceptable downtime for the AI recommendation API?
Guidance: Hours per incident; is the API non-critical (app degrades gracefully) or business-critical?
Notes: Determines need for multi-AZ Lambda, API Gateway redundancy, and health-check alerting

Question: What is the maximum acceptable data loss for listening-history or classification data?
Guidance: RPO in hours; impacts DynamoDB backup frequency and Point-in-Time Recovery (PITR) settings
Notes: Drives backup strategy cost and complexity

Question: What monitoring and alerting is required post-launch?
Guidance: CloudWatch dashboards, Lambda error-rate alarms, latency alarms, cost anomaly alerts
Notes: Defines observability stack scope and who receives alerts (ANP vs. nClouds NOC)

Question: What support coverage is required for the AWS environment after handoff?
Guidance: Business hours only, 24x7 on-call, managed services via nClouds, or self-managed by ANP
Notes: Determines whether a managed-services SOW is needed post-project

Question: Are there preferred maintenance windows for Lambda deployments and infrastructure updates?
Guidance: Days/times with lowest app usage; weekend nights typical for streaming apps
Notes: Impacts CI/CD pipeline deployment gates and change-management process

Question: How do you expect the user base and catalog size to grow over the next 12 months?
Guidance: Active users, monthly uploads, API call volume projections
Notes: Ensures auto-scaling policies and cost estimates remain valid at scale

Question: What log retention period is required for API and application logs?
Guidance: 30 days, 90 days, 1 year — driven by compliance or internal policy
Notes: CloudWatch Logs retention setting; longer retention increases storage cost

Question: What operational training or runbook documentation is required at handoff?
Guidance: Admin guide, incident-response runbook, API onboarding guide for app team
Notes: Defines documentation deliverables for Phase 3 of the engagement

Question: Is this a greenfield AWS deployment with no existing AWS workloads to migrate?
Guidance: Confirm no legacy AWS services need to be decommissioned or migrated
Notes: Greenfield simplifies environment setup; confirms no migration risk

Question: How will the existing Firebase catalog be made available for initial mood classification of the back-catalog?
Guidance: One-time bulk export (JSON/CSV), Firebase REST API batch pull, or manual sample for testing only
Notes: Back-catalog tagging scope directly impacts Phase 2 build timeline and cost

Question: How many existing catalog items require back-fill mood classification at launch?
Guidance: Total count of songs and podcasts needing initial labeling
Notes: Large back-catalogs may require a batch-processing pipeline (Step Functions + Bedrock) rather than on-demand

Question: Is any downtime acceptable on the Firebase/FlutterFlow app side during API integration?
Guidance: Integration is additive (new endpoints), so likely zero downtime — confirm with app team
Notes: Confirms low-risk integration approach and no maintenance window needed for Phase 3

Question: What is the availability of ANP's technical point of contact during the 4–6 week engagement?
Guidance: Hours per week, specific unavailability (holidays, competing projects)
Notes: Resource gaps are a primary schedule risk for a short engagement

Question: Will ANP's technical contact own API testing and UAT, or will nClouds provide test scripts?
Guidance: Define UAT responsibilities: who writes test cases, who executes, who signs off
Notes: UAT ownership affects Phase 3 duration and go-live readiness criteria

Question: Is a separate development/staging environment required, or will a single AWS environment be used?
Guidance: Dev + Prod vs. single environment; impacts cost and deployment pipeline complexity
Notes: Separate environments add ~$50–200/month in base costs but reduce production risk

Question: What format and level of detail is required for the API documentation delivered at handoff?
Guidance: OpenAPI/Swagger spec, Postman collection, README, Confluence page, or all of the above
Notes: Documentation format must be consumable by the FlutterFlow app team without nClouds involvement

Question: What are the go/no-go criteria for production launch of the new API endpoints?
Guidance: Performance benchmarks met, security review passed, UAT sign-off, documentation complete
Notes: Defines exit criteria for the engagement and final acceptance conditions

Question: What knowledge transfer sessions are required for the ANP team at handoff?
Guidance: Architecture walkthrough, ops runbook walkthrough, live demo, recorded session
Notes: Ensures ANP can operate and maintain the solution independently after engagement closes

Question: Are there any dependencies on Firebase APIs or FlutterFlow plugin updates that could block integration?
Guidance: Firebase REST API rate limits, FlutterFlow HTTP package version, CORS policy constraints
Notes: Third-party platform constraints are outside nClouds control and could delay Phase 3

Question: Are there any technology restrictions that would prevent use of specific AWS AI services (e.g., Bedrock, Comprehend)?
Guidance: Legal, procurement, or faith-based policy restrictions on specific foundation model providers
Notes: Some organizations prohibit use of certain LLM providers for ethical or doctrinal reasons

Question: What is the fallback behavior if the AI recommendation or classification endpoint is unavailable?
Guidance: App shows generic playlist, last-known recommendations, or error message to user
Notes: Graceful degradation design is critical for user experience and should be spec'd in Phase 1

Question: Are there any assumptions about the quality or completeness of lyric and transcript text in Firebase?
Guidance: Missing transcripts, non-English content, very short or low-quality text — prevalence estimate
Notes: Poor text quality is the top risk to classifier accuracy; may require a data-quality remediation step
